name: Alembic
what_it_does: Database schema migration tool for SQLAlchemy in Python. Generates migration scripts from model changes (or manual authoring), tracks migration history in an alembic_version table, and applies migrations in order. The standard migration tool for SQLAlchemy-based Python applications and agent data layers.
use_cases: Manage database schema evolution for Python agent services with versioned migration scripts and rollback support
Auto-generate migration scripts from SQLAlchemy model changes using alembic --autogenerate
Apply schema changes to agent production databases safely with up/down migration scripts
Integrate database migrations into Python agent deployment pipelines (alembic upgrade head before app start)
Track migration history across multiple environments (dev, staging, prod) for agent data layer consistency
not_for: Non-SQLAlchemy databases — Alembic is designed for SQLAlchemy ORM; use flyway or liquibase for Java/multi-language
No-SQL databases — Alembic is SQL-schema focused; use MongoDB Atlas App Services for NoSQL schema management
Simple single-file SQLite apps where schema is recreated from scratch — Alembic overhead isn't worth it for throwaway DBs
best_when: You're using SQLAlchemy for your Python agent's data layer and need production-grade database schema migrations with version tracking and rollback.
avoid_when: You're not using SQLAlchemy — use Flyway, Liquibase, or Django's built-in migrations for other ORM/framework migrations.

auth.notes: Migration tool — database auth configured via SQLAlchemy connection URL.

pricing.notes: MIT license. Maintained by Michael Bayer (SQLAlchemy author).

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — migration execution speed depends on schema changes and table sizes.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Migration conflicts (multiple heads) give clear error messages. Database errors during migration include the failing SQL. alembic history and alembic current commands show migration state.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Alembic tracks applied migrations in alembic_version table — running alembic upgrade head is idempotent (skips already-applied migrations).
known_agent_gotchas: autogenerate does not detect all schema changes — custom types, indexes on expressions, and some constraints may not be auto-detected; always review generated migrations before applying
Migration conflicts (multiple heads) occur when branches are created without merging — use alembic merge to create a merge migration before running upgrade
Running migrations in CI/CD requires database access — avoid running migrations in unit tests against production DB; use separate test DB or in-memory SQLite for unit tests
Alembic online vs. offline mode — offline migration scripts generate SQL without DB connection; useful for DBAs who apply SQL manually in production
--autogenerate compares against the current database state, not previous migration — running autogenerate on a fresh DB with no tables generates all tables; running on existing DB detects diffs
Large table migrations (adding columns with defaults, building indexes) lock tables in PostgreSQL/MySQL — use concurrent index builds and zero-downtime migration patterns for production

security_score_components.security_notes: DB credentials in alembic.ini URL — use environment variable interpolation (%(DB_URL)s) rather than hardcoding credentials. Migration scripts run with full DB privileges — use separate migration user with schema-change permissions.
